parts.title: The Autumn Fair
parts.text: Every autumn, Maya helped her family prepare for the **harvest** festival. She would **purchase** sacks of grain from the market at dawn, haggling in her **eloquent** way until the merchants smiled and agreed to her price. The fields were **vast**, stretching so far in every direction that the far edge disappeared into morning mist.

parts.title: The Gathering Storm
parts.text: The **temperature** dropped sharply before noon, and dark clouds rolled in from the hills. Leo, the village elder, was always **cautious** — he studied the sky before deciding anything. He told the farmers to finish quickly and come inside. Maya heard the thunder **croak** and rumble overhead and began to **grumble** quietly, worried the rain would ruin their carefully stacked harvest.

parts.title: The Discovery
parts.text: After the storm, Maya and Luna walked through the **similar**-looking fields that all ran together at the edges of the village. Then Maya spotted something extraordinary: a figure sitting alone under a banyan tree, wrapped in a torn shawl. It was an old storyteller who had been traveling for weeks. Maya felt it was a great **honor** to sit beside him. She **insist**ed that he come inside and eat, and would not take no for an answer.

parts.title: The Lesson
parts.text: Over a warm meal, the storyteller spoke of kingdoms, floods, and the **weapon** of words — how a well-chosen story could stop a war or heal a broken heart. Maya listened without moving, her whole body still. When he finally rose to leave at dawn, Leo said quietly, "You treated a stranger with the same care you give family. That is the oldest wisdom there is." Maya never forgot those words, and she passed the story on every harvest season after.

definitions.meaning1: To buy something. Example: We went to the bookshop to purchase a new dictionary for the classroom.
definitions.meaning2: Something that has been bought. Example: She carried her purchases home in two large paper bags.
definitions.studentChallenge: You will need enough money to ___ a ticket before you can enter the museum.

definitions.meaning1: The gathering of ripe crops from the fields. Example: The apple harvest in October kept the farmers busy for weeks.
definitions.meaning2: To gather or collect crops. Example: The workers helped harvest the corn before the first frost.
definitions.studentChallenge: Every fall, the students visited the farm to help with the pumpkin ___.

definitions.meaning1: A measurement of how hot or cold something is. Example: The temperature outside dropped below freezing, so the pond was covered in a thin layer of ice.
definitions.studentChallenge: The nurse used a thermometer to check the ___ of the student who felt sick.

definitions.meaning1: Great respect and admiration. Example: It was an honor to meet the famous author.
definitions.meaning2: To show respect. Example: We honor our veterans on Memorial Day.
definitions.studentChallenge: The award was given in ___ of her achievements.

definitions.meaning1: Being very careful to avoid danger or mistakes. Example: Be cautious when crossing the busy road.
definitions.studentChallenge: The ___ driver slowed down in the rain.

definitions.meaning1: To say firmly that something must happen. Example: She insisted on paying for dinner.
definitions.studentChallenge: He ___ that he did not break the vase.

definitions.meaning1: Very large in area or amount. Example: The vast desert stretched for miles.
definitions.studentChallenge: The ocean is a ___ body of water.

definitions.meaning1: An object used to fight or defend against someone, such as a sword or shield. Example: In the story, the brave knight carried a shining weapon to protect the kingdom.
definitions.studentChallenge: The knight's most powerful ___ was his sharp silver sword.

definitions.meaning1: To complain in a quiet, unhappy way. Example: The students started to grumble when the teacher announced extra homework over the weekend.
definitions.studentChallenge: My stomach began to ___ loudly because I had skipped breakfast that morning.

definitions.meaning1: Almost the same but not exactly alike. Example: The two kittens looked similar, but one had a white spot on its nose and the other did not.
definitions.studentChallenge: Your painting is ___ to mine because we both chose to paint a sunset over the ocean.

definitions.meaning1: Speaking in a powerful and beautiful way. Example: The president gave an eloquent speech.
definitions.studentChallenge: Her ___ words moved the audience to tears.

definitions.meaning1: To make a deep, rough sound like a frog. Example: The bullfrogs began to croak loudly by the pond as soon as the sun went down.
definitions.studentChallenge: We could hear the frogs ___ from across the pond every evening in spring.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Dad went to the market to ___ fresh vegetables, paying for each item at the stall.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: You will need enough money to ___ a ticket before you can enter the science museum.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The apple ___ in October kept the farmers working from sunrise to sunset for three weeks.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Every fall, the students visited the farm to help gather the pumpkin ___ from the fields.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The ___ outside dropped so far below freezing that the pond turned into a solid sheet of ice overnight.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The nurse placed a thermometer under the sick student's tongue to measure his ___.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: It was a great ___ to shake hands with the famous author whose books she had read ten times.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The school held a ceremony to ___ the students who had shown great courage during the storm.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The ___ hiker checked every step carefully before placing her foot on the slippery mountain path.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Be ___ when crossing the busy road — always look both ways before stepping off the curb.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: She ___ on paying the full bill herself, firmly refusing to let anyone else contribute a single coin.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: He ___ loudly and repeatedly that he had not broken the vase, even when nobody believed him at first.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The ___ Sahara Desert stretches across most of northern Africa, covering an almost unimaginable area of land.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The ocean is a ___ body of water so enormous that ships can sail for weeks without seeing land.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: In the story, the brave knight carried a gleaming ___ — a sword that flashed like silver in the sun.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The archer's most reliable ___ was his longbow, which he had trained with every single day.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The students began to mutter and ___ quietly when the teacher announced a surprise test on Friday.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: My empty stomach started to rumble and ___ noisily during the long bus ride home after I skipped lunch.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The two puppies looked nearly ___, but one had a brown patch on its ear and the other did not.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Your drawing is ___ to mine — we both chose to show a sunset over the ocean with orange and pink clouds.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The student council president gave an ___ speech — her words were so well-chosen that the whole room fell silent.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Her ___ farewell moved the audience to tears, with every sentence carefully chosen and beautifully spoken.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The bullfrogs began to ___ loudly by the pond the moment the sun disappeared below the horizon.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Every spring evening, we could hear the frogs ___ and call to each other from across the water.

secretPassage.instructions: Read the passage below. Fill in each blank with the correct vocabulary word from the word bank.
secretPassage.passage: Every autumn, the village of Greenvale celebrated the _(1)_ festival, gathering ripe fruit and vegetables from the fields in a tradition that had lasted for generations. The villagers felt it was a great _(2)_ to take part, and every family considered it a privilege and a mark of deep respect to help. The fields were _(3)_ — stretching so far in every direction that you could not see the far edge even on the clearest day. Farmer Devi was always _(4)_, checking the sky and testing the soil before deciding which crop to pick next, never rushing or taking chances. She would _(5)_ that everyone wear gloves, firmly refusing to begin work until every helper was properly protected. The _(6)_ that week had dropped sharply, the thermometer showing temperatures far below normal for that time of year. Some younger helpers began to _(7)_, complaining quietly that the cold made the work too hard. But old Rajan, the most respected elder, gave an _(8)_ speech — every word chosen with care and beauty — that reminded everyone why the festival mattered. He said kindness was the greatest _(9)_, a far more powerful tool than any sword or shield. Two young sisters who worked side by side had _(10)_ baskets, nearly alike in shape and color, though one was slightly taller. They heard a bullfrog _(11)_ from somewhere deep in the reeds, its rough, low call echoing across the quiet field. With the money the village earned from selling its crop, it planned to _(12)_ new seeds for the following spring, buying enough to plant the largest field yet.

wordScaleImposter.studentPick: Pick 2 imposters and explain why they are opposites of the vocabulary word.
